title: Vandenboschia × quelpaertensis
intro: Vandenboschia × quelpaertensis, also known as Quelpaert Island spleenwort, is a rare and endangered species of epiphytic fern native to Jeju Island in South Korea. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including treating respiratory issues, inflammation, and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Vandenboschia × quelpaertensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in dried and powdered forms for the treatment of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Vandenboschia × quelpaertensis is a hybrid species within the genus Vandenboschia, a group of epiphytic ferns. It belongs to the family Hymenophyllaceae, a family of small to medium-sized ferns. This hybrid species is native to certain regions in Asia. Its classification as a distinct species is based on its unique morphological features and genetic characteristics. Further research may be required to confirm its classification and determine its exact place within the fern family tree.
